quoteLast: After the bulletin was      issued at 5.30 this evening, the President continued in much      the same condition as during the afternoon, the pulse      varying from 102 to 106, with rather increased force and      volume. After taking nourishment he fell into a quiet sleep      about thirty-five minutes before his death, and while asleep      his pulse rose to 120, and was somewhat more feeble. At ten      minutes after ten o'clock he awoke, complaining of severe      pain over the region of the heart, and almost immediately
title:
The Life and Public Services of James A. Garfield
Twentieth President of the United States, Including Full and Accurate Details of His Eventful Administration, Assassination, Last Hours, Death, Etc., Together with Notable Extracts from His Speeches and Letters
author: Brown, E. E. (Emma Elizabeth), 1847-

quoteFirst: We did not get to camp till
quoteLast: : Meares, with his usual good-heartedness, came out from the tent and helped us in for the last miles or so.
title: South with Scott
author: Mountevans, Edward Ratcliffe Garth Russell Evans, Baron, 1880-1957

quoteTime: thirty-five minutes past ten P. M.
quoteLast: , the President called his family about him, to give them his last earthly advice and bid them his last good-by.
title:
The Ladies of the White House; Or, in the Home of the Presidents
Being a Complete History of the Social and Domestic Lives of the Presidents from Washington to the Present Time—1789–1881
author: Holloway, Laura C. (Laura Carter), 1848-1930

quoteFirst: On July 11th (see Fig. 30), the cotyledon fell from 7.35 A.M. till 10 A.M.; it then rose (rapidly after 4 P.M.) till it stood quite vertically at 8.40 P.M. During the early morning of the next day (12th) it fell, and continued to fall till 8 A.M., after which hour it rose, then fell, and again rose, so that by
quoteLast: it stood much higher than it did in the morning, but was not vertical as on the preceding night.
title: The Power of Movement in Plants
author: Darwin, Charles, 1809-1882; Darwin, Francis, Sir, 1848-1925

quoteLast: , and ascended a little during the night.
title: The Power of Movement in Plants
author: Darwin, Charles, 1809-1882; Darwin, Francis, Sir, 1848-1925

quoteFirst: The apex after thus sinking down from 9.10 A.M. to noon, moved a little to the left; it then rose up and circumnutated in a nearly vertical plane until
quoteLast: On the following day (26th) it was observed from 6.40 A.M. to 5.20 P.M., and within this time it moved twice up and twice down.
title: The Power of Movement in Plants
author: Darwin, Charles, 1809-1882; Darwin, Francis, Sir, 1848-1925
